description: Use this API to return an array of complex values for subsystem three-winding transformers. This API routine was first introduced in release 30.3.2. ierr, xarray = atr3cplx(sid, owner, ties, flag, entry, string)
python_syntax: ierr, xarray = atr3cplx(sid, owner, ties, flag, entry, string)

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: Defines the bus subsystem to be used. (input; -1 by default).

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: A flag indicating owner usage if ownership is a subsystem selection criterion (ignored if sid is negative). (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: ties
parameters.description: A flag indicating which subsystem three-winding transformers to include (ignored if sid is negative). (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: flag
parameters.description: A flag indicating which subsystem three-winding transformers to include. (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: entry
parameters.description: A flag indicating organization. (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: string
parameters.description: An array of NSTR elements specifying NSTR of the following strings indicating the transformer quantities desired. (input; no default allowed).

return_values.name: xarray
return_values.description: An array of NSTR*DIM elements specifying Is an array of at least NSTR columns, each of which contains DIM elements. (output).

sid.description: to instruct the API to assume a subsystem containing all buses in the working case.
valid bus subsystem identifier. Valid subsystem identifiers range from 0 to 11. Subsystem SID must have been previously defined.

ties.description: for interior subsystem three-winding transformers only.
for subsystem tie three-winding transformers only.
for both interior subsystem and tie three-winding transformers.

string.description: Actual measured impedance between the winding 1 and winding 2 buses in pu.
Actual measured impedance between the winding 1 and winding 2 buses in units governed by CZ.
Nominal measured impedance between the winding 1 and winding 2 buses in pu.
Nominal measured impedance between the winding 1 and winding 2 buses in units governed by CZ.
Actual measured impedance between the winding 2 and winding 3 buses in pu.
Actual measured impedance between the winding 2 and winding 3 buses in units governed by CZ.
Nominal measured impedance between the winding 2 and winding 3 buses in pu.
Nominal measured impedance between the winding 2 and winding 3 buses in units governed by CZ.
Actual measured impedance between the winding 3 and winding 1 buses in pu.
Actual measured impedance between the winding 3 and winding 1 buses in units governed by CZ.
Nominal measured impedance between the winding 3 and winding 1 buses in pu.
Nominal measured impedance between the winding 3 and winding 1 buses in units governed by CZ.
Magnetizing admittance in pu.
Magnetizing admittance in units governed by CM.
ZGRND’ Zero sequence winding 1 grounding impedance in pu.
Zero sequence winding 1 grounding impedance in units governed by CZG.
‘ZGRND2’ Zero sequence winding 2 grounding impedance in pu.
Zero sequence winding 2 grounding impedance in units governed by CZG.
‘ZGRND3’ Zero sequence winding 3 grounding impedance in pu.
Zero sequence winding 3 grounding impedance in units governed by CZG.
Zero sequence winding 1 or bus 1 to bus 2 leakage impedance in pu.
Zero sequence winding 1 or bus 1 to bus 2 leakage impedance in units governed by CZ0.
Zero sequence winding 2 or bus 2 to bus 3 leakage impedance in pu.
Zero sequence winding 2 or bus 2 to bus 3 leakage impedance in units governed by CZ0.
Zero sequence winding 3 or bus 3 to bus 1 leakage impedance in pu.
Zero sequence winding 3 or bus 3 to bus 1 leakage impedance in units governed by CZ0.
Zero sequence common neutral grounding impedance in pu.
Zero sequence common neutral grounding impedance in units governed by CZG.
Losses (in MW and Mvar).
Losses (units determined by power output options).

error_codes.description: DIM, and hence the size of XARRAY, is not large enough.
Sequence data not in case (when STRING = ‘ZGRND’, ‘ZG1’, ‘ZG1CZG’, ‘ZG2’, ‘ZG2CZG’, ‘ZG3’, ‘ZG3CZG’, ‘Z01’, ‘Z01CZ0’, ‘Z02’, ‘Z02CZ0’, ‘Z03’, ‘Z03CZ0’, ‘ZNUTRL’, or ‘ZNUTRLCZG’).
